cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Stage an attachment through desktop drop UI and send it to CLI.

One canonical attachment message reaches terminal sent state.
CLI verifies filename, MIME, size, digest and downloaded bytes exactly.
negativeChecks: Service-only staging, metadata mismatch, byte/digest mismatch, or duplicate message fails.

cases.action: Sync App history and wait for the visible reply.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; the CLI peer first follows the isolated App identity and becomes a visible follower contact.
cases.action: Open the exact follower contact and tap the visible follow control in the resulting Direct peer information surface.
exactOracles: Button is visible and enabled.
Backend relationship becomes exactly friend and both directional lists contain the exact peer once.
negativeChecks: Missing/disabled/inert click, backend error, or broad friend/follower acceptance fails.

cases.feature: CLI follow and App unfollow interaction
cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Let CLI follow the App identity, refresh, then tap App unfollow.
exactOracles: CLI follow completes.
App observes exact follower/friend convergence and unfollow returns the relation to none.
negativeChecks: UI-only state, wrong peer, nonconverged backend state, or failed tap fails.

cases.preconditions: The contacts-focused awiki.info run has an isolated App state root, the exact CLI peer is an inbound follower, and no Direct row exists for its current DID or Handle.
cases.action: Open the exact follower row before using identity lookup, recents, local search, or sending a first message.
exactOracles: The visible contact row uses the deterministic nickname and opens one dm:peer-scope:v1 conversation with non-empty peerPersonaId.
The empty Direct remains exact-one by Persona before its first message and subsequent contact re-entry reuses the same canonical conversation ID.
negativeChecks: A pre-existing Direct fixture, Handle/DID title drift, legacy dm:<DID> row, second Persona row, wrong peer, or a disappearing empty conversation fails.

cases.feature: Contact row canonical Direct message closed loop
cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; the exact CLI peer is visible in the App Contacts UI after canonical relationship convergence.
cases.action: Click the exact visible contact row, send one run-unique message through the App, restart, then receive and read one run-unique CLI message.
exactOracles: The exact contact row displays the same nickname-first title as identity lookup and opens the existing dm:peer-scope:v1 conversation for the same Persona.
CLI inbox/history contain exactly one matching sender, receiver, body, type, message id, and thread.
Core conversation summary, visible row/header, timeline message, and Product overlay each have exactly one canonical identity with no dm:<DID> overlay.
Restart preserves one canonical row/message; closed-view incoming unread is exactly +1, restart preserves 1, and opening clears it to 0 without rebound.
negativeChecks: A legacy DID row/overlay, duplicate message/summary/UI row, wrong peer/content/type, nonterminal send, missing restart restoration, or inaccurate unread/read state fails.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Exercise the canonical relationship lifecycle through refreshes and repeated reads.
exactOracles: The exact sequence none→follower→friend→following→friend→follower→none is observed.
CLI status derives the combined state from all directional booleans while independently checking the outbound relationship field; refresh does not invent duplicate or stale relations.
negativeChecks: Any broadened state set, duplicate relation, or stale post-refresh state fails.

cases.preconditions: The full remote awiki.info flow has one canonical Direct and one canonical Group for the isolated App state root.
cases.action: While both conversations are closed, deliver Direct, Group, then Direct messages and restart before inspecting the list.
exactOracles: After each delivery, App projection relative order follows the latest canonical message without duplicating either semantic conversation.
After restart, visible Direct and Group rows have exact nickname/group title, exact latest preview, exact unread badge, and Direct appears before Group.
negativeChecks: Duplicate semantic row, stale row order, wrong title/preview, missing row, or zero-unread badge fails immediately.

cases.feature: Nickname consistency across identity and conversation surfaces
cases.preconditions: The CLI peer publishes a deterministic nickname that differs from its Handle and DID.
cases.action: Resolve the peer, open Direct and Contacts, add the same peer to a Group, observe the system event and receive a Group message.
exactOracles: Identity lookup first visible primary title, Direct row/header, and Contact row all use the exact nickname.
Group member projection, member-added system event, and incoming sender label use the same exact nickname.
negativeChecks: Handle, compact/full DID, Unknown, or any different title shown as the primary name while the nickname is available fails.

cases.feature: Full-Handle fallback consistency across App surfaces
cases.preconditions: A dedicated awiki.info CLI peer has no nickname and has one stable full Handle distinct from its DID and generated user name.
cases.action: Resolve the peer and exercise identity lookup, Direct, Contacts, group member, group system-event, and sender-label surfaces through the App.
exactOracles: Identity lookup, Direct row/header, and Contact row use the exact full Handle when nickname is unavailable.
Group member, member-added system event, and incoming sender label use the same exact full Handle.
negativeChecks: A bare local name, generated user name, compact/full DID, Unknown, mixed fallback, transient wrong first title, or later self-healing fails.

cases.feature: DID-only fallback consistency across App surfaces
cases.preconditions: A dedicated awiki.info actor is resolvable and can participate in product flows without either a nickname or a Handle projection.
cases.action: Open identity lookup, Direct, Contacts, group member, group system-event, and sender-label surfaces for the DID-only actor.
exactOracles: Every scoped App surface uses the DID fallback only when both nickname and Handle are unavailable.
negativeChecks: The actor must not appear as Unknown, a stale generated user name, a stale sender snapshot, or a mixed fallback across App surfaces.

cases.feature: Explicit Profile refresh converges every App display surface
cases.preconditions: The awiki.info peer starts with one nickname distinct from its Handle and DID, then publishes a second run-unique nickname while its Direct, Contact, Group, system-event, and sender-label surfaces already exist.
cases.action: Open the existing Direct, click the visible peer avatar to refresh Profile, then reopen identity lookup, recents, Contacts, group info, the member-added system event, and the existing group sender label.
exactOracles: The peer-info title, Direct App projection, conversation row, chat header, identity lookup, and Contact row converge to the exact new nickname while reusing the same Persona and canonical conversation ID.
The existing group member, member-added system event, and message sender label converge to the same exact new nickname without changing the canonical Group conversation.
negativeChecks: A stale old nickname, Handle/DID/Unknown fallback, mixed old/new surfaces, a second Direct row, changed Persona/conversation ID, or a later name reversion fails.

cases.feature: Cached nickname is correct on the first visible frame
cases.preconditions: The CLI peer nickname has already been resolved into the isolated App profile projection and the Direct conversation survives an App-shell restart.
cases.action: Restart the App shell and open the canonical Direct row through the visible conversation list before any remote profile refresh is requested.
exactOracles: After the target conversation becomes selected, the first non-empty scoped chat-header title is the exact cached nickname.
The same nickname remains stable throughout the post-open observation window.
negativeChecks: A first visible Handle, compact/full DID, Unknown label, duplicate title widget, or later title drift fails immediately instead of waiting for self-healing.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Create a group, add the canonical CLI member, and send through visible App UI.
exactOracles: A read-only resolver preflight yields a non-empty DID; the member action itself still performs at most three visible search submissions, selects the one exact enabled candidate, and then requires the selected add-member action to be enabled before tapping it.
The selected group converges to one canonical conversation id before messaging, with the exact visible group title, empty preview and unread 0.
The empty group row remains exact-one after member refresh and App restart before the first message.
One canonical group and correct member exist, and the visible member-added system event uses the member nickname before Handle/DID fallback.
App send reaches terminal state and CLI observes exactly one message with exact sender/content/group.
negativeChecks: Wrong group/member, duplicate semantic Group row, missing/disappearing empty row, title drift, Handle/DID shown despite an available nickname, direct service shortcut, nonterminal send, or duplicate message fails.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Send a run-unique group message from CLI and navigate to it in App UI.
exactOracles: Closed-view CLI delivery increments only the canonical group unread by exactly one and the visible row shows exact title, preview and unread.
App renders exactly one canonical group message with exact content/sender/group and the visible sender label uses the same nickname as the group identity projection.
Opening the exact row clears only that group unread and stable history requery does not duplicate the message.
negativeChecks: Wrong row/thread, missing/duplicate bubble, incorrect preview/unread, sender Handle/DID drift despite an available nickname, or mismatched content/identity fails.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Compose a group mention through App UI and send it.
exactOracles: The real composer retains exact typed input, exposes the candidate panel from an explicit @ trigger, filters to one exact enabled member, and clears only after send submission.
P9 payload contains exactly the intended member DID and valid range.
CLI projection preserves canonical ID, exact text and structured mention metadata.
negativeChecks: Text-only arrival, wrong DID/range/role, malformed JSON, or duplicate message fails.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Send a structured group mention from CLI to the App.
exactOracles: CLI send succeeds with a canonical message id and exact application/json result type.
App canonical projection preserves target DID/range and exact text.
Mention renders in the correct group message exactly once.
negativeChecks: Substring-only matching, metadata loss, wrong target, or duplicate render fails.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Refresh and requery the group history after bidirectional sends and mentions.
exactOracles: The complete run-owned canonical message set remains exactly once and in exact App/UI order in the intended group after refresh.
No run-owned group message appears in another loaded App conversation projection.
negativeChecks: Loaded cross-conversation leakage, missing/extra/out-of-order message, replay duplicate, or identity/content drift fails.

cases.preconditions: The inbound-focused awiki.info run has an isolated App state root and no Direct row for the exact CLI peer current DID or Handle.
cases.action: Keep the peer conversation closed, send the first Direct message from CLI, open the resulting visible row, then re-enter through identity lookup, recents, and local search.
exactOracles: The first inbound message creates exactly one resolved dm:peer-scope:v1 conversation with one peerPersonaId, exact nickname, preview, unread increment, and canonical message ID.
Opening clears only that unread state, and identity lookup, recents, App-shell rebuild, and local search all reuse the same canonical conversation and exact-one timeline.
negativeChecks: A pre-existing Direct fixture, legacy dm:<DID> row, duplicate Persona row, wrong nickname/preview/unread, missing or duplicate message, alternate entry ID, or restart/search drift fails.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Open a canonical Direct conversation, verify it before the first message and after App restart, then type and send a run-unique message through visible App controls.
exactOracles: Before the first send, the resolved Persona has exactly one canonical Direct row; its visible nickname-first title, empty preview, unread 0, selected chat header, and restart state match exactly.
App send reaches terminal sent state and the visible row preview is the exact sent body.
CLI inbox contains exactly one canonical message with exact body, sender, receiver, thread and type.
negativeChecks: A second row for the same Persona, missing/duplicate empty row, title/preview drift, loss on restart, rejected or nonterminal send, wrong identity/content, zero matches, or duplicate message fails.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Send a run-unique direct message from CLI while the App target conversation is not open, then open it through UI.
exactOracles: CLI send is accepted and has a canonical message ID.
Global and target-conversation unread move from baseline to exactly baseline+1, while the scoped visible row shows the exact preview and localized unread count.
After an App-shell rebuild, the canonical row preserves the exact title/preview/unread and one exact bubble; opening clears list and navigation unread and removes the row badge without rebound.
negativeChecks: Wrong conversation alias, duplicate bubble, incorrect badge delta, premature read, or rebound fails.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Inject the E2E transport failure, tap the visible retry action, reconnect, rebuild the App shell, and requery.
exactOracles: The controlled failed row stays visible in the canonical conversation even when the write path uses a DID alias or a core reset arrives.
The visible retry performs exactly one real remote transport attempt, reports a typed failure code if it fails, and reaches terminal sent state.
The complete run-owned Direct sequence has the exact canonical IDs, contents, conversation ownership and visible order after reconnect and App-shell rebuild.

Two distinct canonical messages with the same body remain exactly two ordered messages during realtime delivery, converge to strictly increasing serverSequence values, and keep that order through the stability window and rebuild.
negativeChecks: Silent success, duplicate/extra/missing/out-of-order message, non-increasing converged serverSequence, cross-conversation leakage, same-body collapse, new duplicate on retry/replay, lost message, or stale failed state fails immediately.

cases.feature: Cross-conversation exact message sequence and isolation
cases.preconditions: The full remote awiki.info flow has one canonical Direct and one canonical Group.
cases.action: Deliver ordered Direct/Group traffic, then hide the App, send a three-message Direct burst without waiting for App convergence, resume, open each row, and requery local history.
exactOracles: The Direct and Group run-owned canonical ids are exact-once and ordered in App projection, visible bubbles, and SDK local history with no cross-conversation leakage.

After hidden/resume, the three-message burst produces exact +3 Direct/global unread and the last-message preview; opening Direct shows the exact burst ID/body/sender order and clears only that unread without rebound.
negativeChecks: Duplicate/missing/extra id, wrong body/sender/conversation, swapped burst order, stale preview, unread drift/rebound, or cross-conversation leakage fails.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Select the daemon and enable Message Agent through visible UI.
exactOracles: Daemon selection, active binding and enabled UI agree.
negativeChecks: Missing/disabled UI, skipped real scenario, wrong runtime identity, duplicate result, or backend/UI divergence fails.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Send from CLI and wait for App/runtime recovery.
exactOracles: Exact source message and one runtime final result are visible in App history.
negativeChecks: Missing/disabled UI, skipped real scenario, wrong runtime identity, duplicate result, or backend/UI divergence fails.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Confirm the visible draft/action and await its result.
exactOracles: Confirmation is attributable to the canonical request and one final result is returned.
negativeChecks: Missing/disabled UI, skipped real scenario, wrong runtime identity, duplicate result, or backend/UI divergence fails.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Revoke Daemon message authorization through the visible confirmation flow.
exactOracles: User Service binding and daemon binding both converge to revoked after the UI action.
negativeChecks: UI-only success, one-sided revoke, missing confirmation, or stale active binding fails.

cases.preconditions: Flutter desktop and debug native IM Core artifacts are available.
cases.action: Provision one Storage Scope through the real App bootstrap, reopen the same native vault, then remove its key and retry openExisting.
exactOracles: Provision reaches ready only after the native VaultRequired validator opens.
A second real App bootstrap reads the identical existing root.
After key deletion, bootstrap returns vault_key_missing and does not recreate the item.
negativeChecks: Native load failure, context/path mismatch, root replacement, or missing-key recreation fails the case.

cases.preconditions: A stable signed macOS Release build uses bundle id ai.awiki.awikime and the production scope-secret service.
cases.action: Provision one unique production scope item, terminate the test App process, launch a separately built process to reopen it, reject duplicate create, then delete it in a third process.
exactOracles: The provision process creates revision 1 only in the production service.
The independent reopen process reads the same revision and createExclusive reports already-exists.
The cleanup process deletes the unique item and confirms missing.
negativeChecks: Ad-hoc identity, dev-service access, restart read failure, silent replacement, or cleanup failure fails the release gate.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Complete the measured App/CLI backend scenario.
exactOracles: All required timing phases complete against the remote product path.
negativeChecks: Missing metric, wrong canonical sample, schema drift, or incorrect budget classification fails.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Prepare the audited remote dataset.
exactOracles: Dataset cardinality and conversation distribution match the plan.
negativeChecks: Missing metric, wrong canonical sample, schema drift, or incorrect budget classification fails.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Measure cold App shell and conversation-list visibility.
exactOracles: Both cold visibility metrics are present and finite.
negativeChecks: Missing metric, wrong canonical sample, schema drift, or incorrect budget classification fails.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Measure snapshot, fast-local and full hydration.
exactOracles: All hydration metrics are present in causal order.
negativeChecks: Missing metric, wrong canonical sample, schema drift, or incorrect budget classification fails.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Measure App send to CLI visibility.
exactOracles: Latency uses the run-owned canonical message and is within the hard budget.
negativeChecks: Missing metric, wrong canonical sample, schema drift, or incorrect budget classification fails.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Measure CLI send to App visibility.
exactOracles: Latency uses the run-owned canonical message and is within the hard budget.
negativeChecks: Missing metric, wrong canonical sample, schema drift, or incorrect budget classification fails.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.

exactOracles: No forbidden full conversation refresh occurs.
negativeChecks: Missing metric, wrong canonical sample, schema drift, or incorrect budget classification fails.

cases.action: Open and hydrate the long audited thread.
exactOracles: Open/load metrics are present and within the hard budget.
negativeChecks: Missing metric, wrong canonical sample, schema drift, or incorrect budget classification fails.

cases.action: Write the product timing report.
exactOracles: Required metrics, units, source and run identity are complete.
negativeChecks: Missing metric, wrong canonical sample, schema drift, or incorrect budget classification fails.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Evaluate hard performance budgets.
exactOracles: A hard-budget breach makes the suite fail.
negativeChecks: Missing metric, wrong canonical sample, schema drift, or incorrect budget classification fails.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Evaluate soft performance budgets.
exactOracles: A soft breach is recorded as warning without hiding hard failures.
negativeChecks: Missing metric, wrong canonical sample, schema drift, or incorrect budget classification fails.

cases.preconditions: Remote endpoints are exactly https://awiki.info and wss://awiki.info/im/ws; isolated App and CLI identities pass canonical preflight.
cases.action: Retain sanitized failure diagnostics.
exactOracles: Diagnostics identify the metric/budget without secrets or local paths.
negativeChecks: Missing metric, wrong canonical sample, schema drift, or incorrect budget classification fails.

cases.preconditions: The runner can launch two independent Flutter integration-test processes against one isolated App state root and one attested x86_64 build.
cases.action: Phase A creates Direct/Group conversations, messages, unread state, and cached Profiles; Phase B starts a new Flutter process and restores only persisted state.
exactOracles: The second process restores Persona/Group semantic exact-one, exact message order, unread state, and cached first-visible names without reusing in-memory providers.
negativeChecks: Same-process Widget rebuild, duplicate/lost rows, unread drift, message replay, Handle/DID flash, changed native content hash, or a different state root fails.

cases.preconditions: Flutter desktop and debug native IM Core artifacts are available.
cases.action: Launch the real Flutter desktop app shim without backend credentials.
exactOracles: App shell and onboarding are visible exactly once.
The onboarding authentication entry is visible; macOS exposes the single phone, email, and identity-credential method selector.
Unauthenticated realtime remains disconnected.
negativeChecks: A missing shell/onboarding/authentication-entry surface or an unexpected realtime connection fails the case.

cases.feature: Multi-conversation unread isolation and read clearing
cases.preconditions: The full remote awiki.info flow has one closed canonical Direct and one closed canonical Group.
cases.action: Receive two Direct messages and one Group message, restart, open Group, then open Direct.
exactOracles: Before opening, Direct unread is exactly 2, Group unread is exactly 1, and global unread increases by exactly 3.
Opening Group clears only Group and leaves Direct at 2; opening Direct then restores the exact baseline total with no rebound during the stability window.
negativeChecks: Wrong per-row/global count, clearing the wrong row, lost restart state, duplicate badge, or delayed unread rebound fails.

cases.feature: Canonical Direct conversation across App entries
cases.preconditions: The awiki.info peer has a nickname distinct from its Handle and DID, and the isolated App state contains no Direct row for that Persona.
cases.action: Create the Direct through identity lookup before the first message, then reopen it through recents, local conversation search, restart, and the visible Contact row.
exactOracles: Every App entry reuses the same peer-scope canonical conversation ID and peer Persona ID.
The empty pre-message conversation remains visible, then messages, preview, unread, and cached nickname stay attached to that single row across restart.
negativeChecks: A legacy dm:<DID> row, a second semantic row, a changed Persona/conversation ID, a lost empty row, or entry-specific title/state fails.

cases.feature: Canonical Group conversation across App lifecycle
cases.preconditions: The App creates a unique group on awiki.info through visible UI.
cases.action: Create the group, add the canonical peer, refresh members, restart the App shell, and send/receive the first messages.
exactOracles: Group creation produces exactly one conversation whose conversation ID, canonical Group DID, and wire thread remain aligned before the first message.
Adding a member, refreshing, restarting, and sending messages preserve that same single Group row.
negativeChecks: A disappearing empty group, duplicate Group row, changed Group identity, member-add replacement row, or first-message-only resurrection fails.
